content: Standard vanilla-JS event bus: a class holding a Map/object of eventName -> Set of callback subscribers, with subscribe() returning an unsubscribe function and publish()/emit() iterating subscribers synchronously. Alternative: use a plain DOM node (e.g. a document.createDocumentFragment() or dedicated EventTarget instance) with addEventListener/dispatchEvent(CustomEvent), leveraging the native EventTarget class instead of hand-rolling pub/sub. Best practices: namespace event names (module:action) to avoid collisions, always provide unsubscribe/cleanup, keep the bus a single module-scoped singleton so producer (signal.js) and consumer (inference.js) never import each other directly.
